seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI chatbots with OpenAI Agents SDK enables sophisticated multi-agent systems with handoffs, tools, and tracing for conversational AI. However, complex agent interactions introduce risks like infinite loops, prompt injections, and unvalidated tool outputs that can degrade reliability. Vex provides essential output guardrails to ensure response accuracy, safety, and coherence in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Chatbot uses OpenAI Agents SDK to orchestrate specialized agents: an IntentClassifierAgent routes user queries, RetrievalAgent fetches RAG context from vector stores, ResponseGeneratorAgent crafts answers with conversation memory, and SafetyAgent performs final validation. Agents handoff via the SDK's structured protocol, maintaining thread state for multi-turn coherence. Tools integrate external APIs while memory management persists context across sessions.

This modular design scales conversations but amplifies failure modes during handoffs. Tracing reveals bottlenecks, but lacks runtime verification for accuracy and safety. Vex integrates as output parsers on each agent to catch issues before propagation.

The high-risk profile demands verification for hallucinations, PII leaks, and coherence across agent boundaries.

risks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
risks.description: IntentClassifier repeatedly routes to RetrievalAgent without progress, consuming tokens indefinitely
risks.mitigation: Vex loop detection guardrail on agent outputs flags repetitive handoff patterns

risks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
risks.description: Malicious users inject instructions that override SafetyAgent instructions during handoffs
risks.mitigation: Vex PII/safety filtering validates all agent outputs regardless of prompt content

risks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
risks.description: RetrievalAgent returns irrelevant/malformed RAG context that propagates hallucinations
risks.mitigation: Vex response accuracy checks validate tool outputs before ResponseGenerator consumption

risks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
risks.description: OpenAI SDK tracing misses intermediate agent states, obscuring failure root causes
risks.mitigation: Vex captures structured evaluations at every handoff for complete observability

implementation.title: Intent Classification Agent Setup
implementation.description: Routes user input to appropriate specialized agents using OpenAI Agents SDK
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from vex import Vex

intent_agent = Agent(
    name="IntentClassifier",
    instructions="Classify user intent: 'retrieval', 'general', 'safety_check'. Return only the intent type.",
    model="gpt-4o",
    tools=[]
)

runner = Runner()

@vex.guardrail(
    verifications=[
        "response_accuracy",
        "safety_filtering"
    ]
)
def classify_intent(message: str) -> str:
    result = runner.run_sync(
        intent_agent,
        message,
        thread_id="chat_1"
    )
    return result.final_output

user_intent = classify_intent("Find me flight information to NYC")

implementation.title: RAG Retrieval Agent with Tool Integration
implementation.description: Fetches relevant context from vector store for response generation
implementation.code:
from agents import tool

@tool
async def search_knowledge_base(query: str):
    """Search vector store for relevant context"""
    # Pinecone/Vector DB integration
    results = pinecone_index.query(
        vector=embeddings.embed_query(query),
        top_k=5
    )
    return [match.text for match in results.matches]

retrieval_agent = Agent(
    name="RetrievalAgent",
    instructions="Use search_knowledge_base for user queries. Return ONLY relevant context passages.",
    tools=[search_knowledge_base]
)

@vex.guardrail(
    verifications=[
        "response_accuracy",
        "hallucination_detection"
    ]
)
def retrieve_context(intent: str, query: str) -> list:
    result = runner.run_sync(
        retrieval_agent,
        f"Intent: {intent}. Query: {query}",
        thread_id="chat_1"
    )
    return result.final_output

implementation.title: Response Generator with Memory
implementation.description: Generates coherent responses using conversation history and RAG context

implementation.title: Safety Agent Final Validation
implementation.description: Performs PII detection and content moderation before user delivery

vexIntegration.title: Loop Detection Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Prevents infinite agent handoff cycles by tracking state patterns

vexIntegration.title: PII Protection with Redaction
vexIntegration.description: Scans and redacts sensitive information from all agent outputs

vexIntegration.title: Multi-Verification Pipeline
vexIntegration.description: Combines all verifications in single output parser for agent chains
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import VerificationPipeline

chatbot_pipeline = VerificationPipeline([
    "response_accuracy:0.85",      # 85% confidence threshold
    "conversation_coherence",
    "safety_filtering:high",
    "pii_protection",
    "hallucination_detection:strict"
])

@chatbot_pipeline
@vex.guardrail()
def full_chatbot_pipeline(user_message: str, history: list) -> dict:
    # Complete agent workflow
    intent = classify_intent(user_message)
    context = retrieve_context(intent, user_message)
    response = generate_response(context, history, user_message)
    validated = validate_final_response(response)
    
    return {
        'response': validated['redacted_response'],
        'approved': validated['approved'],
        'vex_score': chatbot_pipeline.score
    }
